story.text: As the loop, I need a foreign reviewer's response to land as value-gated atoms under one proposed arc, because today the envoy only pushes outward (new through seal) and a returned review strands in read-only docs/sources or as report prose the queue never sees - which is retro 0037 one layer up, the loop running beside the work. The inbound seam (envoy respond) closes the return leg: one finding becomes one proposed atom, grouped so a single confirm-arc queues the whole review, with provenance back to the package and its seal, and a response_received receipt on the disclosure ledger.

_note: The backing atom for the inbound envoy seam (done-line 0057). Built, tested with section-10 teeth, and demonstrated by routing the qa-metabolism review through it as first light. This atom is the work itself on the log, so the PR that lands the seam is atom-backed and walks the gates rather than bypassing them (the atom-invariant, retro 0037). This session authored the seam, so it cannot sign this atom's value line - an independent node judges it. Never mutated: the fold over .ai-native/log/ is the live state (D-5).
